raw_entries.text:
January 3rd
Started the new year determined. I'm going to finally finish the app.
Told myself: no distractions. Focus only. I've said this before but this 
time it's different. Spent 6 hours on Twitter instead of coding. Whatever.

January 9th
Michael from the startup offered me a co-founder role. 10% equity.
I said I'd think about it. I've been thinking about it for 5 days.
The longer I wait, the more I convince myself it's a bad idea.
Classic me. I did the same thing with the Lagos fintech job in 2022.

January 14th
Didn't reply to Michael. He went with someone else. 
I told everyone he was a bad founder anyway.
Deep down I know I was scared.

January 22nd
New job going well on paper. Got praised by the CTO.
But I've been checking my phone all day. Can't focus for more than 20 minutes.
I have 3 unfinished side projects on my laptop. 4 including the one I quit last month.

February 1st
Argument with Temi again. She said I go cold when I'm stressed.
She's right but I told her she was being dramatic.
I hate that I do that. I can't seem to stop.

February 8th
Someone on Twitter called my side project a knockoff.
I deleted the whole project from GitHub. 12 weeks of work. Gone.
I know it was stupid. But I couldn't take it.

February 15th
My boss asked me if I want to lead the new product vertical.
Huge opportunity. More money. More visibility.
I said I needed to think about it. I always say I need to think about it.
My thinking usually means: I'm going to find reasons to say no.

February 28th
Haven't touched my laptop in 2 weeks outside of work.
Been playing FIFA every evening. Telling myself I need rest.
I know I'm hiding. I always hide after a failure, real or imagined.

March 5th
Called my brother about the product lead role.
He said "just take it." Simple.
But I'll probably wait until the deadline has passed.
That's what I do. I wait. Then the decision is made for me.
Then I blame circumstances.

March 12th
Temi broke up with me. She said she can't love someone who isn't there
even when they're in the room. She's not wrong.
I shut down emotionally every time something goes wrong at work.
She deserved better.

March 15th
Still haven't given my boss an answer about the product lead role.
The deadline is tomorrow. I haven't slept properly.
I keep thinking: what if I fail? What if people realize I can't do it?

January: ₦45,000 on gaming subscriptions and gadgets, ₦2,000 on books
February: ₦38,000 on food delivery (mostly late nights), ₦0 on courses or learning
March: ₦52,000 on entertainment and clothes
Savings this quarter: ₦0

January - March patterns:
- 14 "deep work" blocks scheduled. 2 completed.
- 6 meetings rescheduled due to "not feeling ready"
- 3 networking events RSVP'd. 0 attended.
- Side project sessions scheduled every weekend. Skipped 9 of 12 weekends.
